title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends on fuel transition, shipyard actions, government, ports, financing and industry players
The U.S. Department of Transportation's Federal Transit Administration (FTA) has announced $384.4 million in federal funding from the Biden-Harris Administration to expand and improve ferry service in communities across the country and accelerate the transition to zero-emission transportation. Read More

Looking forward
It was early October 2021 when IMF stressed that global economic upswing was continuing, even as the pandemic resurged.

children.summary:
Department of Transportation's Federal Transit Administration (FTA) has announced $384.4 million in federal funding from the Biden-Harris Administration to expand and improve ferry service in communities across the country and accelerate the transition to zero-emission transportation. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-swoxpjW35J0.jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-swoxpjw35j0_63909df6afe1.jpg

children.title: Looking forward
children.summary: It was early October 2021 when IMF stressed that global economic upswing was continuing, even as the pandemic resurged.

children.title: Why the dichotomy?
children.summary:
Equity and bond markets continue to rally, with the FTSE 100 hitting all-time highs last week. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-vrSKrUEZsDY.jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-vrskruezsdy_f83b148edbac.jpg

children.title: Slow start to the year
children.summary:
The Baltic Panamax index time-charter equivalent declined by $368/day today, printing at $8,894/day at the time of writing, as dry bulk markets move past the holiday period in the Far East. Read More
Image reference: unnamed (25).jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unnamed282529_7c559de52eff.jpg

children.title: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
section_path: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.section_path_text: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.summary:
The first half of February provides a weaker trend in freight rates, while the number of ballasters continues to weigh on freight market sentiment, as the imbalance between supply and demand diminishes the chances of an early recovery. Read More
Image reference: unnamed (26).jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unnamed282629_32471d11a645.jpg
Image reference: unnamed (27).jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unnamed282729_4191d008b634.jpg

children.title: Expectations of lower inflation in 2023
children.summary:
Signs of improving demand boosted sentiment across commodity markets. Read More
Image reference: unnamed (28).jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unnamed282829_79f9da7c18d0.jpg

children.title: Remaining a significant issue in China is that the housing market continues to deteriorate.
section_path: Remaining a significant issue in China is that the housing market continues to deteriorate.
children.section_path_text: Remaining a significant issue in China is that the housing market continues to deteriorate.
children.summary: Some positive sentiment was spreading in the dry bulk market a few weeks ago regarding recent improvement in Chinese excavator sales and robust savings among Chinese citizens, but there still is a grim likelihood that existing housing supply far exceeds both current demand and also any higher level of demand that could come this year.

children.title: Previously, stockpiles had decreased for two straight weeks
section_path: Previously, stockpiles had decreased for two straight weeks
children.section_path_text: Previously, stockpiles had decreased for two straight weeks
children.summary: China's commodity stockpiles have been rising. 136.5 million tons of iron ore is now stockpiled at Chinese ports, which is 3.1 million tons (2%) more than was stockpiled a week ago.

children.title: Crude oil edged lower
children.summary:
Markets were mixed as traders balanced stronger demand in China against the prospects of weaker economic growth in the US. Read More
Image reference: unnamed (31).jpg | assets/2023-02-12_weekly-insights-review_img_unnamed283129_06de34802c81.jpg

children.title: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
section_path: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.section_path_text: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary: In recent days, the US dollar has given up some of the gains recorded during the past week and on Monday.
